properties.Test Case: On a page with an Image Description, change zoom; ensure ID doesn't become hidden.
properties.Steps: On a page with an Image Description, change zoom; ensure ID doesn't become hidden.

properties.Test Case: Make a new page. Type "Two |words". If it isn't already open, open the Talking Book Tool. Now go to another page and come back. Or, more efficiently, just click on the thumbnail of the current page to cause it to reload. The | should appear.
properties.Steps: Make a new page. Type "Two |words". If it isn't already open, open the Talking Book Tool. Now go to another page and come back. Or, more efficiently, just click on the thumbnail of the current page to cause it to reload. The | should appear.

properties.Test Case: Now close the TB Tool. The "|" should disappear. Again, reload the page repeatedly, it should stay disappeared.
properties.Steps: --- Now close the TB Tool. The "|" should disappear. Again, reload the page repeatedly, it should stay disappeared.

properties.Test Case: Import multiple text boxes on a page in a mixture of stereo and mono. Test playback in ePUB and Bloom Reader etc. (JT: The google folder has "Various audio rates.bloomSource" which has audio in three rates and both mono and stereo for a quick smoke test. You might try adding more from the website mentioned in the book. Currently, their "short" different-rate samples are not short. I truncated them using commands like ffmpeg -i ff-16b-1c-11025hz.mp3 -t 16 -c copy gs-ff-16b-1c-11025hz.mp3.)
properties.Steps: Import multiple text boxes on a page in a mixture of stereo and mono. Test playback in ePUB and Bloom Reader etc. (JT: The google folder has "Various audio rates.bloomSource" which has audio in three rates and both mono and stereo for a quick smoke test. You might try adding more from the website mentioned in the book. Currently, their "short" different-rate samples are not short. I truncated them using commands like ffmpeg -i ff-16b-1c-11025hz.mp3 -t 16 -c copy gs-ff-16b-1c-11025hz.mp3.)

properties.Test Case: Import multiple text boxes on a page in a mixture of sample rates, e.g. 44100Hz and 48000Hz. Test playback in ePUB and Bloom Reader etc.
